references.title: Deep learning approach for predicting university dropout: a case study at Roma Tre University
references.journal: Journal of E-Learning and Knowledge Society

extracted.Courses: Courses in the Department of Education of Roma Tre University between 2009 and 2014
extracted.Features: [Year of beggining, Year of Birth, Gender, Country of Birth, Highschool Type, Highschool Score, Year ending highschool, Transfer, ECTS from other university, Faculty, Academic Year, Course Code, Course Name, Course Year, Family Income Class, Working Status, Exemption from Taxes, Handicap, PArt Time Status, Part Time ECTS, Type of enrollment renew]
extracted.Preprocessing Details: Model is capable of making predictions that the end of academic year. There is one model for each year.
extracted.Comments:
Authors attempt to do prediction of performance of the student at the start of the academic year.

This is Model B, the one using the feature sets of “immutable data”.

Authors also use Bayesian Models with default parameters. Results are little unintelligible though.
extracted.Limitations:
Small dataset, especially for Deep Learning purposes.

No model optimization is performed.

Unclear reason for the different sizes in Validation and Test Dataset.

Impractical model to use (1 model per year), becomes computationally expensive.

extracted.Courses: Courses in the Department of Education of Roma Tre University between 2009 and 2014
extracted.Data sources:
Pre-Admission Data,
Academic Records in University
extracted.Features: [Year of beggining, Year of Birth, Gender, Country of Birth, Highschool Type, Highschool Score, Year ending highschool, Transfer, ECTS from other university, Faculty, Academic Year, Course Code, Course Name, Course Year, Family Income Class, Working Status, Exemption from Taxes, Handicap, PArt Time Status, Part Time ECTS, Type of enrollment renew, Exam Name, Score of Exam, Maximum Score of Exam, ECTS of Exam, Exam Date, Academic Year, Type of Validation]
extracted.Preprocessing Details: Model is capable of making predictions that the end of academic year. There is one model for each year.
extracted.Comments:
This is model A, the set that includes ALL Features and is the model that outperformed the remaining options.

Best F1 is achieved with all features
extracted.Limitations:
Small dataset, especially for Deep Learning purposes.

No model optimization is performed.

Unclear reason for the different sizes in Validation and Test Dataset.

Impractical model to use (1 model per year), becomes computationally expensive.

annotations.text: Around 11% of EU young adults end up droppoing out of tertiary-education, thus prediting early should be regarded as a priority. Deep learning models, powered by CNNs have been shown to contribute significantly to more accurate models. The study in question takes administrtative data from 6000 students and results show an accuracy of 67% in dropout for first-year students and 94% for third-year students.
